_comment: Tron sandbox seccomp profile — closes the residual H1 sandbox-hardening item. Stricter than Docker's default by removing syscall classes the Python/bash payload demonstrably doesn't need: mount, swap, kexec, ptrace, and namespace-creation syscalls. Apply via security_opt in tron/services/sandbox_client.py::_hardened_run_kwargs. If you need to debug why a payload is failing, set TRON_SANDBOX_SECCOMP=disabled to drop back to the Docker default temporarily, fix what you found, then re-enable. See docs/security/SANDBOX_THREAT_MODEL.md for what each removed syscall would have allowed.

syscalls._comment: Generally needed for any process. Includes Python interpreter syscalls, file I/O, network read syscalls (under network_mode=none these reach a netns with no NIC anyway), thread/process control, signal handling. Lifted from Docker's default profile and trimmed.

_removed_from_default: _comment: Syscalls Docker's default profile DOES allow but we remove because the Tron sandbox payloads (Python script execution, bash commands) demonstrably don't need them. Each removal is a hardening step.
mount: container shouldn't be able to mount additional filesystems
umount2: same
pivot_root: changes the root filesystem — only init needs this
chroot: not needed; rootfs is already isolated
kexec_load / kexec_file_load: load a new kernel — never legitimate from a sandbox
ptrace: process tracing; lateral-move vector if multiple sandbox containers ever shared a pidns (we don't, but defense in depth)
swapon / swapoff: change kernel swap config
personality: change execution personality (legacy compat tricks)
modify_ldt: x86 segment descriptor manipulation
iopl / ioperm: I/O port manipulation
reboot: containers shouldn't reboot the host
setdomainname / sethostname: rejected to prevent log spoofing
init_module / finit_module / delete_module: kernel module loading
lookup_dcookie: rare, kernel-internal
name_to_handle_at / open_by_handle_at: filesystem handles for nfsd
perf_event_open: perf subsystem
process_vm_readv / process_vm_writev: cross-process memory access
userfaultfd: user-space page-fault handling, kernel exploit vector
bpf: eBPF program loading
io_uring_setup / io_uring_enter / io_uring_register: io_uring has had multiple severe CVEs and isn't needed for our workload
